mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profitability, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with UNRATE remaining below 4.5% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the federal funds rate indicates a tightening monetary policy, which raises the cost of capital and reduces liquidity, leading to negative impacts on equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points month-over-month for two consecutive months.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no statistically significant negative reaction to triggered FEDFUNDS rate hikes over a 3-year period, or are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales combined with consistent job creation indicates strong consumer demand and a healthy economy, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 returns higher.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 100,000 persons.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS show strong growth, SPX 20d returns are flat or negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals increasing manufacturing activity and business investment, indicating strong economic momentum that supports future corporate earnings and equity prices, particularly during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: In early-cycle expansions, periods of high INDPRO growth do not lead to significantly positive 60d SPX returns, with a t-test p-value > 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates weakening residential investment and broader economic demand, signaling potential economic contraction that leads to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% month-over-month.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances following a >10% month-over-month drop in HOUST over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, allows central banks to maintain accommodative monetary policy, which supports asset valuations by keeping discount rates low and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with CPIAUCSL remaining below 2.5% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and an increasing federal funds rate signals a severe economic downturn or stagflationary environment, where slowing growth is met with tightening monetary policy, severely pressuring equity markets and leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS is above 2.5%.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises significantly while FEDFUNDS is elevated, SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 30% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates a robust and expanding economy, which typically translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns. This positive effect is particularly strong during periods of accelerating growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 3.0%.
failure_condition: During periods of accelerating growth, SPX 5d returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with high GDPC1 growth, with a t-test p-value > 0.1.
